pattern_context.pattern_description: Detects public/external functions that modify privileged state without access restrictions
pattern_context.why_flagged: Function setOwner is public and modifies the owner state variable (privileged state) without any access control modifier. Anyone can call this function and become the owner.

steps.red_flag: Function is public - anyone can call it
No modifier on line 25 - no access restriction

questions_to_answer: What privileged state does it modify?
What is the impact if anyone can call it?

test_context.attack_scenario:
1. Attacker identifies unprotected setOwner function
2. Attacker calls setOwner(attacker_address)
3. Attacker becomes the new owner
4. Attacker can now use all owner-only functions
test_context.expected_outcome: Any address can become owner without restriction

similar_exploits.name: Ronin Bridge Hack
similar_exploits.summary: Compromised private keys due to weak access control over validator nodes

fix_recommendations: Add onlyOwner modifier: function setOwner(address newOwner) public onlyOwner
Consider two-step ownership transfer for additional safety
